map.title: True Size of Countries Map: Compare Real Country Sizes
map.description: Drag countries on an interactive world map to compare their real size. See how Mercator projection distorts Greenland, Africa, Russia, the USA, and more.

globe.title: Countries on a True Globe — Size on Planets
globe.description: Spin the orthographic globe to compare countries at true scale and drop them on other planets.

equator.title: Equator Lab — Custom Mercator Projection
equator.description: Tilt the equator and explore how a custom Mercator projection reshapes countries and distortion.

seaLevel.title: Sea Level Rise Interactive Map
seaLevel.description: Simulate sea level rise on a satellite world map or 3d globe map, online simulator for sea level rise.

greenland-vs-africa.title: Greenland vs Africa: True Size Map Comparison
greenland-vs-africa.description: Compare the true size of Greenland and Africa on an interactive map and see why Mercator maps make Greenland look much larger than it is.
greenland-vs-africa.h1: Greenland vs Africa: True Size Comparison
greenland-vs-africa.intro: Greenland often looks close to Africa on a Mercator world map, but Africa is far larger in real area. Drag Greenland on the map to see how latitude changes its apparent scale.
greenland-vs-africa.takeaway: Africa is roughly 14 times larger than Greenland by land area, even though Mercator projection makes Greenland look enormous near the Arctic.

faq.question: Is Greenland really almost as large as Africa?
faq.answer: No. Greenland is about 2.17 million square kilometers, while Africa is about 30.37 million square kilometers.

faq.question: Why does Greenland look so big on many maps?
faq.answer: Mercator projection stretches areas near the poles, so high-latitude places like Greenland appear much larger than their true area.

russia-vs-united-states.title: Russia vs United States: True Size Map Comparison
russia-vs-united-states.description: Compare the true size of Russia and the United States on an interactive world map and see how latitude affects their apparent size.
russia-vs-united-states.h1: Russia vs United States: True Size Comparison
russia-vs-united-states.intro: Russia spans far northern latitudes, so Mercator maps exaggerate parts of it. Compare Russia with the United States at true geographic scale.
russia-vs-united-states.takeaway: Russia is about 1.7 times the area of the United States, but Mercator projection can make the visual difference look even larger.

faq.question: How much larger is Russia than the United States?
faq.answer: Russia is about 17.1 million square kilometers, compared with about 9.8 million square kilometers for the United States.

faq.question: Does Mercator projection exaggerate Russia?
faq.answer: Yes. Because much of Russia is far north, Mercator projection stretches its apparent area on flat world maps.

canada-vs-united-states.title: Canada vs United States: True Size Map Comparison
canada-vs-united-states.description: Compare Canada and the United States at true scale and see how Mercator projection changes their apparent size.
canada-vs-united-states.h1: Canada vs United States: True Size Comparison
canada-vs-united-states.intro: Canada and the United States are close in total area, but Canada appears more inflated on Mercator maps because it reaches farther north.
canada-vs-united-states.takeaway: Canada is only slightly larger than the United States by total area, but Mercator projection makes northern Canada look much bigger.

faq.question: Is Canada much larger than the United States?
faq.answer: No. Canada is about 9.98 million square kilometers and the United States is about 9.83 million square kilometers.

faq.question: Why does Canada look so large on world maps?
faq.answer: Canada extends into high northern latitudes, where Mercator projection stretches land area.

australia-vs-greenland.title: Australia vs Greenland: True Size Map Comparison
australia-vs-greenland.description: Compare Australia and Greenland on a true size map and see why Greenland looks misleadingly large on Mercator maps.
australia-vs-greenland.h1: Australia vs Greenland: True Size Comparison
australia-vs-greenland.intro: Australia is far larger than Greenland, even though Greenland can look comparable on a Mercator world map.
australia-vs-greenland.takeaway: Australia is about 3.6 times larger than Greenland by area. Greenland looks oversized on Mercator maps because it sits near the pole.

faq.question: Is Australia larger than Greenland?
faq.answer: Yes. Australia is about 7.69 million square kilometers, while Greenland is about 2.17 million square kilometers.

faq.question: Why do Australia and Greenland look similar on some maps?
faq.answer: Mercator projection preserves local shape but inflates high-latitude places like Greenland.

india-vs-united-states.title: India vs United States: True Size Map Comparison
india-vs-united-states.description: Compare India and the United States at true scale on an interactive world map.
india-vs-united-states.h1: India vs United States: True Size Comparison
india-vs-united-states.intro: Drag India or the United States across the map to compare their true area and see how latitude changes apparent scale.
india-vs-united-states.takeaway: The United States is about three times the area of India, but apparent size still changes as each country moves north or south on a Mercator map.

faq.question: How large is India compared with the United States?
faq.answer: India is about 3.29 million square kilometers, while the United States is about 9.83 million square kilometers.

faq.question: Does India get distorted much on Mercator maps?
faq.answer: India is closer to the tropics than many northern countries, so its Mercator distortion is smaller than places near the poles.

brazil-vs-united-states.title: Brazil vs United States: True Size Map Comparison
brazil-vs-united-states.description: Compare Brazil and the United States at true scale on a draggable Mercator map.
brazil-vs-united-states.h1: Brazil vs United States: True Size Comparison
brazil-vs-united-states.intro: Brazil and the United States are both continental-scale countries. Compare them at true size and see how their map scale changes with latitude.
brazil-vs-united-states.takeaway: The United States is somewhat larger than Brazil by total area, while Brazil's lower latitude means it is less exaggerated on Mercator maps.

faq.question: Is Brazil almost as large as the United States?
faq.answer: Yes. Brazil is about 8.52 million square kilometers, compared with about 9.83 million square kilometers for the United States.

faq.question: Why does Brazil often look smaller than northern countries?
faq.answer: Mercator projection inflates high-latitude countries more than countries near the equator.

russia-vs-canada.title: Russia vs Canada: True Size Map Comparison
russia-vs-canada.description: Compare Russia and Canada on a true size map and see how Mercator projection affects both northern countries.
russia-vs-canada.h1: Russia vs Canada: True Size Comparison
russia-vs-canada.intro: Russia and Canada both sit far north, so both are visually inflated on Mercator world maps. Compare their true areas on the interactive map.
russia-vs-canada.takeaway: Russia is about 1.7 times larger than Canada, and both countries are exaggerated by Mercator projection because of their northern latitude.

faq.question: Is Russia larger than Canada?
faq.answer: Yes. Russia is about 17.1 million square kilometers, while Canada is about 10.0 million square kilometers.

faq.question: Are both Russia and Canada distorted on Mercator maps?
faq.answer: Yes. Both extend far north, where Mercator projection inflates apparent area.

japan-vs-united-states.title: Japan vs United States: True Size Map Comparison
japan-vs-united-states.description: Compare Japan and the United States at true scale on an interactive Mercator map.
japan-vs-united-states.h1: Japan vs United States: True Size Comparison
japan-vs-united-states.intro: Use the true size map to compare Japan and the United States, then drag each country to see how latitude affects apparent map scale.
japan-vs-united-states.takeaway: The United States is about 26 times the area of Japan. Moving countries across latitudes shows how Mercator distortion changes their displayed scale.

faq.question: How large is Japan compared with the United States?
faq.answer: Japan is about 378 thousand square kilometers, while the United States is about 9.83 million square kilometers.

faq.question: Can I drag Japan on the map?
faq.answer: Yes. Select Japan in the comparison set and drag it to another latitude to see how its apparent Mercator scale changes.
